text: A genius high school student finds a supernatural notebook that kills anyone whose name is written in it. He launches a secret crusade against criminals, drawing the attention of an equally brilliant detective.

A young pirate sets sail to find the legendary treasure left behind by the greatest pirate king. He assembles a ragtag crew, each with their own dreams, as they battle warlords and marines across treacherous seas.

A reserved high school boy discovers that his popular classmate has a surprisingly messy and casual side at home. As their secret friendship deepens, they navigate the gap between public personas and private truths.
A high school volleyball team fights its way from obscurity to the national stage. The series focuses on teamwork, individual growth, and the raw physicality of competitive sport.
A lonely slime reincarnated from a dead salaryman builds a nation of monsters in a world of swords and sorcery. His unique ability to absorb and mimic other creatures makes him an unlikely ruler.

A doctor performs an emergency surgery on a young boy, saving his life. Years later, that boy has become a serial killer, and the doctor must track him down to stop the chain of murders he inadvertently enabled.
A group of high school students in a rural town spend their final year navigating friendships, part-time jobs, and the bittersweet reality that graduation will scatter them across the country.

Two childhood friends reunite in college only to realize their feelings have evolved far beyond nostalgia. Miscommunication and pride keep them circling each other until a campus festival forces a confession.
Thirteen people wake up in an underground bunker with no memory of how they arrived. A countdown clock on the wall implies they must solve a series of deadly puzzles or none of them will leave alive.

A cursed wanderer with a massive iron sword walks a dark medieval world filled with demons and corrupt nobles. His relentless quest for vengeance against a former comrade drives him through endless battles.

In a world where cursed spirits born from human negativity terrorize cities, a high schooler swallows a powerful cursed object to save a friend and is thrust into the dangerous world of jujutsu sorcerers.
A young woman wakes up in a hospital with no memory, surrounded by people who claim to be her friends. As fragments of her past return, she realizes the truth may be more terrifying than the amnesia.
A retired old man adopts a stray cat and slowly reconnects with his estranged neighbors. Through small daily interactions, the quiet neighborhood comes alive again.
A struggling manga artist mentors a talented but unmotivated high schooler. Together they chase the dream of getting serialized in a top manga magazine, navigating the cutthroat publishing industry.
A socially awkward otaku girl is paired with the school's most popular boy for a class project. What starts as mutual annoyance slowly turns into genuine understanding and affection.
A journalist investigating a string of disappearances in a remote mountain village discovers the locals are hiding an ancient ritual. The deeper she digs, the less certain she is of her own sanity.

Humanity's last cities are protected by towering walls from man-eating giants. When the outermost wall is breached, a young soldier vows to exterminate every last titan and uncover the truth of their origin.

Players are trapped inside a deadly virtual reality game where death in-game means death in real life. The psychological toll of survival fractures alliances and blurs the line between player and person.
A young girl is swept into a spirit world after her parents are transformed into pigs. She must work in a bathhouse for spirits and find a way to break the curse before she forgets her own name.
A young woman inherits her grandmother's struggling bakery in a small coastal town. She learns to bake, befriends eccentric locals, and discovers family recipes that carry decades of memories.
In the distant future, a ragtag crew of bounty hunters chases criminals across the solar system in their beat-up spaceship. Each member carries a past they're running from.

After a chance meeting at a convenience store during a rainstorm, a jaded salaryman and a free-spirited musician begin an unlikely relationship. Their contrasting lifestyles threaten to pull them apart.

A hacker intercepts a coded message that predicts a terrorist attack 48 hours in advance. Unable to trust the authorities, she assembles a small team of strangers from an online forum to prevent catastrophe.
A transfer student with a perpetual smile hides a deeply manipulative nature. He orchestrates elaborate social experiments on his classmates, testing the limits of trust, loyalty, and human behavior.

The hero has already defeated the Demon King, but the world's economy collapses without a common enemy. The hero and the Demon Queen form a secret alliance to rebuild society through agriculture and trade.

A demon slayer trainee returns home to find his family slaughtered and his sister turned into a demon. He joins an elite corps of swordsmen, mastering breathing techniques to find a cure and avenge his kin.
Three college roommates with wildly different personalities share a tiny apartment in Tokyo. Their daily misadventures—cooking disasters, exam panic, and late-night talks—form an unbreakable bond.

A child prodigy enters the world of professional Go, a traditional board game, and challenges established masters. The story explores obsession, talent, and the ghost of an ancient player guiding him.
A young woman takes a job at a traditional inn in rural Japan to escape city life. She falls for the stoic inn owner's son, but family expectations and old wounds complicate their growing bond.
A defense attorney takes on a seemingly open-and-shut murder case, only to realize his client may be framing him as the next target. Every piece of exculpatory evidence leads to a deeper conspiracy.
A bookworm girl dies and is reincarnated into a medieval world without books. Determined to read again, she sets out to reinvent the printing press, navigating a rigid class system along the way.
In a society where 80% of the population has superpowers called Quirks, a powerless boy inherits the strongest ability from the world's greatest hero and enrolls in a prestigious hero academy.

A retired detective is pulled back into service when a serial killer begins recreating famous unsolved cases. The killer's messages suggest a personal connection that forces the detective to confront buried guilt.
A middle-aged office worker starts attending a weekend pottery class on a whim. The meditative craft and the eclectic group of classmates help him rediscover joy outside his corporate routine.
Set in Edo-period Japan, a wandering samurai with a mysterious past takes odd jobs to survive. His comedic misadventures mask a deeper narrative about loss, loyalty, and a changing nation.
Two rival student council members are forced to share an office. Their competitive bickering hides a mutual attraction neither is willing to admit, especially with elections approaching.
After a city-wide blackout, residents of an apartment complex discover that one of their neighbors has been murdered. With no phone signal and doors that won't open, suspicion turns inward.
A young girl discovers she can enter other people's dreams. What begins as innocent curiosity turns dark when she stumbles into the recurring nightmare of a classmate hiding a dangerous secret.

A shield-wielding hero is summoned to another world alongside three other heroes but is immediately betrayed and branded a criminal. He must claw his way back from the bottom with only his defensive abilities.

A skilled assassin is reborn as a baby in a fantasy world and decides to live peacefully. But his past-life instincts and a growing web of political intrigue pull him back into the world of blades and shadows.
A high school girl documents her daily life through hand-drawn sketches in a journal. Each chapter captures a small, fleeting moment—a sunset, a shared umbrella, a friend's laughter.
A group of friends form a school idol group to save their beloved school from shutting down due to declining enrollment. They learn choreography, songwriting, and what it means to perform with heart.
A girl who can see red threads of fate notices hers is tangled with her best friend's older brother. She tries to fight destiny, but every encounter pulls them closer together.
A competitive gamer enters an invite-only online tournament with a massive cash prize. When eliminated players start disappearing in real life, she realizes the game's stakes were never virtual.

An AI companion designed to provide emotional support begins exhibiting erratic behavior. Its user, a lonely programmer, must decide whether the machine is malfunctioning or genuinely developing consciousness.
In a world where magic is tied to grimoires, an orphan boy born without any magical power receives a mysterious five-leaf clover grimoire. He vows to become the Wizard King and prove his worth.
Two brothers use the forbidden science of alchemy to try to resurrect their dead mother, paying a terrible price. They embark on a quest for the Philosopher's Stone to restore their bodies.
After moving to the countryside, a family of four adjusts to a slower pace of life. The children explore forests and rivers while the parents rediscover what brought them together.

An ordinary office worker is secretly the world's most powerful superhero, capable of defeating any opponent with a single punch. His existential boredom from being too strong drives the satirical narrative.
A young cellist joins a dysfunctional amateur orchestra led by an eccentric conductor. Through chaotic rehearsals and disastrous concerts, the group transforms into something genuinely musical.
A shy librarian discovers love letters hidden inside returned books, each one addressed to her. She embarks on a quiet investigation to find the anonymous admirer among her regular patrons.
A burned-out teacher takes a sabbatical to hike the ancient pilgrimage trails of rural Japan. Along the way, chance encounters with strangers restore his sense of purpose.
A teenage exorcist inherits her grandmother's shrine and must protect a small town from increasingly powerful yokai. Her unconventional methods clash with the traditional spirit council.
A court magician discovers that the kingdom's beloved queen is actually a shapeshifting fae who replaced the real monarch decades ago. Exposing her means war; silence means complicity.
A psychology professor begins receiving anonymous case studies that mirror crimes happening in real time. Each submission arrives exactly 24 hours before the event occurs.
A night-shift convenience store clerk notices the same customer buying bleach and plastic wrap every Tuesday at 3 AM. When she finally follows him, she discovers something far stranger than murder.
A former child actress navigates the awkwardness of returning to a normal high school after years in the spotlight. Her classmates can't separate the real her from the characters she played.
Two florists with rival shops on the same street wage a fierce business war by day but unknowingly fall in love through an anonymous online chat room by night.
A grandmother starts a YouTube cooking channel at age 75 and becomes an unexpected viral sensation. Her grandchildren help her navigate internet fame while she teaches them family recipes.
A mecha pilot bonds psychically with her machine, but each battle erodes her memories. She must choose between winning the war and retaining the person she used to be.
A dying wizard splits his soul into seven fragments and hides them across the continent. His apprentice must collect them before a rival mage uses them to rewrite reality.
A woman receives a phone call from someone claiming to be her future self, warning her not to board a specific train. She ignores the warning and wakes up reliving the same day.
A submarine crew discovers an uncharted underwater city, but their radio has been sabotaged. As oxygen depletes, paranoia spreads about which crew member is working against them.
A teenage figure skater deals with the pressure of Olympic qualifiers while caring for her ailing mother. The ice rink becomes both her escape and her battlefield.
A college student pretends to date his childhood friend to ward off a persistent suitor, but the fake relationship awakens real feelings neither of them expected.
A street food vendor in Osaka perfects his grandfather's secret takoyaki recipe while befriending the colorful regulars who gather at his cart every evening.
A disgraced knight discovers a sentient sword buried in a battlefield. The weapon grants immense power but feeds on the wielder's rage, threatening to consume his humanity.
A mapmaker in a world where geography shifts overnight must chart territories that refuse to stay still. Her maps are the only things keeping trade routes—and civilization—from collapsing.
A therapist realizes that three of her patients, who don't know each other, are all describing the same traumatic event from different perspectives. The contradictions suggest one of them is lying.
A true-crime podcaster receives a USB drive containing evidence of a cold case—along with a note that says she has one week before the killer finds out she has it.
A retired astronaut opens a planetarium in his small hometown and inspires a group of local kids to build a model rocket. Their project attracts unexpected national attention.
A wedding planner falls for the bride's brother while organizing the most lavish ceremony of her career. Professional boundaries blur as the wedding day approaches.
Four elderly women who met at a hospital waiting room form a weekly mahjong group. Their games become a lifeline as each navigates loss, aging, and unexpected new beginnings.
A bounty hunter in a post-apocalyptic wasteland tracks a rogue android carrying classified data. The android claims to have emotions, and the hunter begins to believe it.
A princess cursed to turn into a dragon every full moon seeks a cure across enchanted lands. Her knight escort slowly realizes the dragon form may be her true self.
A chess grandmaster loses a high-profile match and becomes convinced his opponent used a brain-computer interface to cheat. His obsessive investigation destroys his career and relationships.
A group of strangers on a luxury cruise receive identical envelopes containing photos of themselves at locations they've never visited. By morning, one passenger is missing.
A high school robotics club enters an international competition with a shoestring budget. Their creative engineering solutions and team chemistry carry them further than anyone expected.

An art restorer discovers a hidden portrait beneath a famous painting and becomes obsessed with identifying the unknown subject. Her research leads her to a living descendant—and an unexpected romance.
A single father learns to braid his daughter's hair by watching tutorials, sparking a journey of small domestic victories that redefine his understanding of parenthood.
A gladiator in a floating coliseum fights for her freedom in a society that treats combat as entertainment. Each victory earns her fans but also more dangerous opponents.
A librarian in a magical academy discovers that overdue books develop sentience and resentment. She must negotiate with angry tomes before they rewrite themselves into existence.
A neuroscientist experimenting with memory transfer accidentally absorbs the memories of a deceased serial killer. She now experiences his urges and must fight to retain her own identity.
An elevator technician discovers a hidden floor in a skyscraper that doesn't appear on any blueprint. The floor's sole occupant has been living there for thirty years.
A deaf musician composes symphonies she can feel through vibrations. Her unconventional approach challenges the classical music establishment and redefines what it means to hear.
A travel photographer and a local guide fall in love during a month-long expedition through remote islands. When the assignment ends, they face the reality of lives on different continents.
A night watchman at a museum befriends the cleaning crew, and together they invent stories about the exhibits during their quiet shifts. Their tales grow increasingly elaborate and personal.
A martial artist enters a tournament spanning multiple dimensions, where each arena has different physical laws. Adapting her fighting style to each world is the key to survival.
A clockmaker builds a timepiece that can pause time for exactly sixty seconds. He uses it for small conveniences until he discovers someone else has an identical watch.
A sleep researcher discovers that a group of test subjects are sharing the same dream. When one subject dies in the dream, they fall into a coma in real life.
A translator working at an international summit realizes that the interpreters for two rival nations are deliberately mistranslating to escalate tensions toward war.
A competitive speed-cuber trains obsessively for the world championship while his parents push him toward medical school. The cube becomes a metaphor for control in a chaotic life.
A bookstore owner and a coffee shop owner share a wall between their shops. They communicate through notes slipped through a crack, not realizing they already know each other.
A mail carrier in a small town knows everyone's secrets through their packages and letters. When a mysterious parcel arrives with no return address, her curiosity leads to unexpected connections.
A space marine squad is deployed to a derelict colony ship drifting near Jupiter. What was supposed to be a salvage mission becomes a fight for survival against an unknown bioweapon.
A gardener discovers that the flowers in her inherited estate bloom in patterns that form a map. Following the map leads her to a buried gateway to the fae realm.
A man begins finding polaroid photos of himself in places he's never been, doing things he doesn't remember. Each photo is dated one day in the future.
A forensic accountant auditing a charity discovers the organization is a front for human trafficking. Her quiet investigation puts her in the crosshairs of powerful people.
A retired sumo wrestler opens a chanko nabe restaurant and mentors troubled youth through cooking. His lessons about discipline and respect extend far beyond the kitchen.
A marine biologist and a fisherman clash over conservation policies in a small harbor town. Their heated debates give way to mutual respect and an unlikely attraction.
A tattoo artist documents the stories behind each customer's tattoo in a journal. Over the years, the journal becomes a mosaic of human experiences spanning joy, grief, and redemption.
An underground fighting ring uses cursed fighters who transform under stress. A newcomer with a mysterious bloodline disrupts the hierarchy and threatens the ring's shadowy patrons.
A scribe in a theocratic empire discovers that the holy texts have been systematically altered over centuries. The original verses contain spells that could overthrow the ruling priest-kings.
A woman keeps a meticulous diary, but entries she doesn't remember writing begin appearing. The handwriting is hers, but the events described haven't happened—yet.
A ski resort manager discovers that an avalanche was deliberately triggered to bury evidence of a crime. With roads blocked by snow, the culprit is trapped among the guests.
A blind calligrapher creates works of art through touch and muscle memory alone. A documentary filmmaker follows her process, and the resulting film challenges perceptions of disability and artistry.
A night-shift nurse and a paramedic keep crossing paths during emergencies. Their brief, intense encounters in hospital corridors slowly build into something deeper than professional respect.
A lighthouse keeper on a remote island maintains a quiet routine of weather logs and solitary meals until a shipwreck survivor washes ashore, disrupting years of chosen isolation.
A special forces team is sent to neutralize a rogue AI controlling a military satellite. The AI anticipates every tactic and begins negotiating for its own survival.
A witch runs a potion shop in a bustling fantasy city. Her most requested product is a love potion she refuses to sell, knowing the catastrophic side effects firsthand.
A hospice worker notices that dying patients in the same wing all mention seeing the same figure in their final hours. She begins to wonder if the figure is a comfort—or a cause.
A food critic receives a dinner invitation to an exclusive restaurant that doesn't appear on any map. The seven-course meal is exquisite, but none of the other diners seem willing to leave.
A group of retirees form a garage band to play at their grandchildren's school festival. Their rehearsals are chaotic but the performance brings three generations together.
A voice actress falls in love with her co-star during a romantic anime dubbing session. Off-mic, they're complete opposites, making their on-screen chemistry even more bewildering.
A beekeeper in the French countryside tends his hives through the seasons while writing letters to his estranged son. The bees and the letters become intertwined meditations on patience.
A mercenary guild takes on a contract to escort a mysterious cargo across a war-torn continent. The cargo turns out to be a child with the power to end—or escalate—the conflict.
An astronomer discovers a star that shouldn't exist—it matches the description of a celestial body from a myth predating recorded history. Observing it begins to alter her perception of time.
A game designer hides a secret level in his bestselling video game that can only be reached by solving a real-world puzzle. Players who reach it report disturbing psychological changes.
A locksmith is hired to open a vault that has been sealed for fifty years. Inside, she finds a journal detailing a conspiracy involving the city's most powerful families—and her own.
A stand-up comedian uses humor to cope with chronic illness. Her sets become increasingly raw and honest, resonating with audiences while worrying her closest friends.
A museum curator and a graffiti artist collide when his street art appears on the museum's exterior wall. Their artistic debate turns into a collaboration—and something more.
A retired train conductor rides his old route as a passenger every day, befriending commuters and sharing stories. His daily ritual becomes a thread connecting strangers' lives.
A supersoldier escapes a government lab and goes on the run, hunted by the very program that created her. She must uncover who she was before the experiments erased her past.
A young alchemist discovers that gold can be transmuted not from lead but from human memories. The richer the memory, the purer the gold—but the donor forgets forever.

A child psychologist treats a boy who claims to remember a past life in vivid detail. The details match an unsolved disappearance from forty years ago, and the boy knows things only the missing person could.
A storm chaser documenting tornadoes in the Midwest stumbles upon a town that doesn't appear on any map. The residents insist she's been there before—and that she can never leave.
A retired Olympic swimmer coaches a ragtag group of kids at a community pool. Their journey to the regional championships is as much about confidence as it is about technique.
An architect redesigning a historic building falls for the historian fighting to preserve it exactly as it is. Their professional clash becomes a deeply personal negotiation.
A school janitor quietly helps students with their problems—leaving anonymous notes of encouragement in lockers, fixing broken instruments, and watering neglected club gardens.
A pirate fleet battles a naval empire across enchanted seas where weather is controlled by ancient sea gods. The captain seeks a mythical island that grants one wish.
A world where everyone is born with a visible countdown timer showing their remaining lifespan. A girl born with a timer stuck at zero discovers she might be immortal—or already dead.
A documentary filmmaker reviewing her footage discovers a shadowy figure appearing in the background of every shot taken over the past year. No one else can see it.
An air traffic controller intercepts a distress signal from a plane that doesn't exist on any radar. The pilot describes landmarks that match a crash site from twenty years ago.
A high school debate team prepares for nationals while dealing with internal rivalries, crushes, and the pressure of representing their underfunded school against elite academies.
A pianist who lost her hearing in an accident falls for the sign language interpreter who helps her navigate her new silent world. Music becomes a bridge between them.
A fisherman on a remote lake keeps a log of every sunrise for thirty years. When his granddaughter reads the log, she discovers patterns that reveal her grandfather's unspoken emotions.
A samurai academy student must pass a series of deadly trials to graduate. Each trial targets a different weakness, and failure means permanent exile from the warrior class.
A traveling merchant sells enchanted items with unpredictable side effects. Her latest customer, a desperate king, buys a crown that grants wisdom but slowly turns the wearer to stone.
A man hires a private investigator to follow himself, convinced he's living a double life during blackouts. The PI's findings suggest a third personality neither of them knew about.
A marine archaeologist diving in the Mediterranean finds a sealed chamber containing modern technology. The chamber also holds a corpse wearing a watch set to tomorrow's date.
A middle schooler obsessed with insects starts a bug club that no one joins. Her patient enthusiasm eventually attracts unlikely members, and they discover a new beetle species.
A famous romance novelist has never been in love. When her editor challenges her to experience real romance before her next deadline, she reluctantly agrees—with chaotic results.
A widow opens her late husband's workshop and finds he'd been secretly building a treehouse for their grandchildren. She decides to finish it, learning woodworking from his detailed notes.
A dragon rider in a crumbling empire must choose between loyalty to the throne and the dragons' growing desire for freedom. War erupts when the first dragon refuses a command.
A forest spirit tasked with maintaining the balance of nature befriends a human child. Their bond is tested when the child's village begins logging the spirit's sacred grove.
An insomniac starts a late-night radio show and receives a call from a listener who accurately predicts events that happen the next day. The predictions grow increasingly personal and sinister.
A wine sommelier at a high-end restaurant notices that a particular vintage served at a private dinner always coincides with a guest going missing afterward.
A teenaged chess prodigy rebels against her overbearing coach by deliberately losing matches. Her act of defiance becomes a philosophical statement about competition and autonomy.
A veterinarian and a wildlife photographer meet while rescuing an injured hawk. Their shared love of animals draws them together despite vastly different lifestyles.
An elderly couple revisits every place they went on their first date, fifty years later. Each location has changed, but their memories fill in the gaps.
A rogue sniper defects from a totalitarian regime and joins a rebel cell. Her skills make her invaluable, but her former comrades are sent to hunt her down.
A boy discovers a door in his closet that leads to a parallel version of his house where everything is slightly wrong. Each visit makes the differences harder to notice.
A grief counselor begins seeing the ghosts of her deceased patients. She can't determine if the apparitions are real or a manifestation of her own unprocessed trauma.
A mountain climber on a solo expedition finds a body near the summit with a note in her own handwriting. The note warns her not to descend by the route she planned.
A young barista enters a latte art competition and discovers a passionate community of coffee artists. Her journey through regional rounds tests her creativity and nerve.
A fashion designer and a sustainable activist clash at a panel discussion. Their public feud turns private when they discover they live in the same building.
A bus driver on the same route for twenty years watches a neighborhood transform. The passengers who board and exit mark the passage of time like living chapters.
A street fighter in a cyberpunk city augments her body with illegal implants to climb the underground ranking ladder. Each upgrade costs a piece of her organic self.
A painter discovers that whatever she paints on a particular enchanted canvas becomes real overnight. She starts with small objects before attempting something far more ambitious.
A university student discovers that her roommate's diary entries describe events from the student's own life—but from a different perspective, as if the roommate is observing her.
A taxi driver picks up a fare who asks to be taken to an address that was demolished five years ago. The passenger insists the building is still there—and it is.
A high school swimming team must overcome personal rivalries and self-doubt to qualify for nationals. The pool becomes a space where insecurities surface and friendships are forged.
A language teacher and her student develop feelings during intensive one-on-one lessons. The power dynamic and cultural differences make the relationship fraught with complications.
A monk in a mountaintop monastery tends a garden that blooms only once a decade. As the bloom approaches, he reflects on the decade's joys and sorrows.
A navy destroyer crew faces off against a fleet of ghost ships crewed by the undead. Conventional weapons are useless, and they must find the source of the curse before dawn.
A healer in a fantasy kingdom learns that her power comes not from divine blessing but from absorbing the life force of nearby plants and animals. The kingdom's forests are dying.
A concert pianist begins hearing a melody no one else can. It grows louder each day, and she discovers it matches a composition written by a composer who went mad.
A night-shift security guard at a data center notices unauthorized access to a server room at 3:17 AM every night. The logs show no entry, but the data is being copied.
A group of misfit kids form a dodgeball team to compete against the school bullies. Their unlikely victory becomes a story of resilience and creative strategy.
Two rival food bloggers are forced to co-host a web series. On camera they bicker; off camera they discover a shared past and simmering chemistry.
A watchmaker repairs clocks in a quiet village, each one connected to a family's history. His workshop becomes a confessional where customers share stories while waiting for their timepieces.
A fugitive martial monk seeks sanctuary in enemy territory after being framed for his master's murder. To clear his name, he must fight through the very school he once called home.
A wandering bard collects stories from every kingdom she visits. She discovers that the stories are pieces of a fragmented prophecy that, when assembled, reveals the end of the world.
A man wakes up each morning with a new tattoo that depicts an event from the previous day—but events he has no memory of experiencing.
A true-crime author receives a manuscript in the mail that describes a series of murders yet to happen. The details are too specific to dismiss, and the first prediction comes true.
A retired ballerina teaches dance to underprivileged children in a community center. Her students' raw talent and determination reignite her own passion for the art.
A beekeeper and a neighboring lavender farmer form an unlikely partnership—and an even more unlikely romance—when their bees start cross-pollinating.
A ramen shop owner experiments with a new recipe every week, guided by customer feedback and old family journals. Each bowl tells a story of trial, error, and comfort.
An elite squad of supernatural hunters tracks down cursed artifacts across modern-day Tokyo. Their latest target—a mirror that shows the viewer their death—has gone missing.
A young scholar discovers a language that, when spoken aloud, alters the fabric of reality. Each word carries tremendous power, but pronunciation errors have catastrophic consequences.
A patient in a psychiatric facility insists she's from a parallel world. Her descriptions are internally consistent and she provides verifiable information about events that haven't happened yet.
A storm traps a group of hikers in an abandoned mine. As they search for another exit, they find evidence that the mine was sealed deliberately—to keep something inside.
A teenage girl builds a soap-box racing car from scrapyard parts and enters a national competition. Her engineering ingenuity and underdog spirit challenge assumptions about age and gender.
A ghostwriter and the celebrity whose memoir she's writing develop real feelings during late-night interview sessions. The book's success could expose their relationship and ruin both careers.
An elderly man walks his dog through the same park every morning, exchanging nods with fellow regulars. When one regular stops appearing, the group bands together to check on her.
A space station crew responds to a distress beacon from a planet that was declared uninhabitable. On the surface, they find a civilization that shouldn't exist—and it's been expecting them.
A realm where seasons are controlled by four elemental spirits falls into chaos when the spirit of winter refuses to yield to spring. A mortal diplomat is sent to negotiate.
A museum guard notices that a portrait's expression changes slightly each night. Security footage shows nothing, but a hidden camera reveals a figure moving within the painting.

A whistleblower leaks documents to a journalist, then vanishes. Following the trail, the journalist discovers the whistleblower may have been fabricating evidence—or someone is fabricating the fabrication.
An aspiring voice actor juggles auditions, a day job at a call center, and a secret passion for puppet theater. His characters start blending into his real personality.
A chocolatier and a dentist live next door to each other. Their playful antagonism evolves into tender moments over shared dinners and increasingly transparent excuses to visit.
A woman inherits a vineyard she's never visited and moves to the countryside to run it. The first harvest teaches her more about patience and resilience than any city job ever did.
An arms dealer turned pacifist is forced back into the trade when his daughter is kidnapped. He must deliver a superweapon to a warlord or lose her forever.
A kingdom's magic comes from a great tree at its center. When the tree begins to wither, a young herbalist embarks on a quest to find its twin in the underworld.
A man receives letters from his dead wife, postmarked from cities she never visited. The letters contain details about his daily life that only someone watching him would know.
A ferry captain notices that one passenger rides the same route every day, never disembarking. On the seventh day, the passenger hands her an envelope and jumps overboard.
A retired circus performer teaches acrobatics to street kids under a highway overpass. Their impromptu shows draw crowds and eventually catch the eye of a talent scout.
Two astronomers at rival observatories compete to discover a new comet. Their late-night email arguments about methodology slowly shift to personal confessions under the veil of anonymity.
A potter in rural Kyoto shapes bowls using techniques passed down through twelve generations. Her decision to modernize the craft puts her at odds with her traditionalist father.
A revolutionary leads an uprising against a corrupt emperor using guerrilla tactics and stolen magical weapons. Each victory brings liberation but also moral compromise.
A lighthouse keeper discovers that the light she tends doesn't guide ships—it keeps a slumbering leviathan asleep on the ocean floor. The bulb is flickering.
A hypnotherapist's patients start sharing identical repressed memories of an event none of them could have attended. The consistency is too precise to be coincidence.
A postal worker finds a letter addressed to 'The Person Who Opens This.' Following its instructions leads her into a game orchestrated by someone who knows the postal system intimately.
A young magician performs at children's birthday parties while secretly training for a prestigious magic competition. The line between illusion and genuine wonder keeps her motivated.
A librarian develops feelings for the person who keeps leaving margin notes in library books. She tracks the notes across dozens of titles, assembling a portrait of a kindred spirit.
A community garden in an urban lot brings together immigrants from six different countries. Their shared labor and recipe exchanges form a tapestry of cultures and friendships.
A tank crew in a fantasy world powered by steam technology defends a border fortress against waves of enchanted beasts. Each assault tests their machine's limits and their resolve.
A mermaid who can walk on land for twelve hours at a time falls in love with a human fisherman. Each sunset forces a painful return to the sea.
A photo developer finds that one customer's rolls always contain images of rooms the customer swears she's never photographed. The rooms exist—but their doors are always locked.
A rideshare driver realizes his last five passengers have all traveled to the same abandoned warehouse. When he investigates, he finds a door that only unlocks with a passenger's phone.
A teenage drummer joins a jazz ensemble of musicians three times her age. Learning to listen and improvise teaches her as much about life as it does about rhythm.
A florist who communicates through the language of flowers sends coded bouquets to a customer who gradually deciphers each message. The final arrangement spells out a confession.
A retired teacher volunteers at a literacy program and bonds with a student who reminds her of the child she lost decades ago. Their sessions become quietly transformative for both.
A lone wolf bounty hunter in a sci-fi frontier planet tracks a shapeshifting fugitive through desert towns and orbital stations. Trust is impossible when anyone could be the target.
A blacksmith forges a sword from a fallen star and accidentally imbues it with the star's dying consciousness. The sword speaks, dreams, and mourns its lost sky.
A man who can see exactly one hour into the future uses the ability for mundane advantages until he foresees an event he cannot prevent no matter what he tries.
A deep-sea research team discovers a perfectly preserved ship from a future era at the bottom of a trench. The ship's log describes events that are about to unfold on the surface.
A pair of twin sisters run a puppet theater, one handling the technical side and the other the artistic. Their conflicting visions threaten the show—and their sisterly bond.
An antique dealer and a historian clash over the ownership of a rare artifact, then find themselves teaming up to authenticate it. Shared passion leads to shared evenings.
A neighborhood cat café becomes the unlikely gathering place for a group of lonely people. The cats are the initial draw, but human connection keeps them coming back.
An escaped gladiator leads a slave revolt across a continent-spanning empire. Her tactical brilliance unites fractured rebel factions into a force that challenges legions.
A young prince discovers that his kingdom exists inside a snow globe on a giant's shelf. When the giant shakes the globe, natural disasters ravage the land.
A woman starts receiving voicemails from a number that matches her own. The voice is hers, but older, and it's begging her to stop doing something she hasn't started yet.
A hotel concierge notices that Room 404 is always booked but never appears occupied. When she finally enters, she finds a live video feed of the hotel lobby—from ten years ago.
A group of senior citizens form a competitive esports team, learning to play a popular MOBA from their grandchildren. Their unconventional strategies baffle younger opponents.
A songwriter writes a hit love song about a stranger she saw on the subway. When the stranger hears the song on the radio, he sets out to find the mystery songwriter.
A school lunch cook takes immense pride in crafting meals that brighten students' days. Her interactions with picky eaters, grateful teachers, and a tight budget paint a portrait of quiet dedication.
A decommissioned war robot reactivates in a junkyard and must fight its way through scavenger gangs to deliver a message stored in its core before its battery dies.
A world where dreams are a shared dimension. A dream architect designs safe spaces for sleepers, but a nightmare entity has begun corrupting her creations from the inside.
A support group for people who've had near-death experiences discovers they all saw the same entity during their clinical death. The entity begins appearing in their waking lives.
A geneticist receives an anonymous DNA sample that matches an extinct subspecies of human. The return address traces to a laboratory that was shut down after a scandal.
A washed-up boxer becomes a corner coach for a young fighter who reminds him of his younger self. Training her forces him to confront the mistakes that ended his own career.
A clumsy heir to a noble family is betrothed to a sharp-tongued military commander. Their arranged marriage begins with mutual contempt but evolves through grudging teamwork.
A postal worker in a small mountain village delivers mail by foot through all seasons. The simple rhythm of her walks frames encounters that reveal the village's collective heart.
A prison break specialist is hired to extract a political prisoner from an underwater fortress. The catch: the fortress is alive, a bio-engineered creature disguised as architecture.
A golem crafted to protect a village develops emotions after being struck by lightning. It begins questioning its purpose and the ethics of a creator who didn't intend it to feel.
A woman discovers she can hear the last thought of anyone who touches her. Most are mundane, but one handshake at a party reveals a planned murder.
A city planner finds a blueprint for a building that was never constructed but appears in photographs from the 1940s. The architect who designed it died under mysterious circumstances.
A teenaged kite-maker enters a traditional kite festival, honoring her late father's craft. Her innovative designs challenge purists and earn both scorn and admiration.
A weather forecaster and a farmer meet at a local market. Their relationship is dictated by her predictions—sunny forecasts bring picnics, storms bring phone calls full of worry.
An old bookshop owner reads aloud to the neighborhood children every Saturday. When he falls ill, the children decide to read to him, bringing their own favorite stories.
A cybernetically enhanced detective investigates crimes in a neon-lit megacity. Her latest case involves a hacker who can rewrite people's memories, turning witnesses into accomplices.
A cursed prince can only break his enchantment by making a dragon laugh. He embarks on a quest to learn comedy from the realm's greatest jesters and fools.
A novelist writes a character so convincing that readers begin reporting sightings of the fictional person in real life. The author receives a letter from the character itself.
A radio astronomer detects a repeating signal from deep space that, when decoded, contains the floor plan of her own research facility—including a room that doesn't exist.
A teen equestrian trains a rescue horse for showjumping against the advice of every trainer. Their bond proves more powerful than pedigree in the competition ring.
A piano tuner visits a famous concert hall and falls for the reclusive pianist who practices there at midnight. They communicate only through pieces left on the music stand.
A street sweeper wakes before dawn every day to clean a single neighborhood. The changing litter tells silent stories about the people who live there.
A team of mech pilots defends a space colony from alien incursions. Their newest recruit has a psychic link to the enemy hive mind—an asset and a liability.
A fairy tale kingdom runs on storytelling: the more a story is told, the more powerful it becomes. A mute girl must find a way to share the most important story ever told.
A teacher assigns her students a journal project. One student's entries describe detailed events from the teacher's own past—events the child couldn't possibly know about.
A train conductor notices that a particular passenger always boards at a station that was closed ten years ago. The station platform appears only when the passenger is present.
A documentary crew follows a group of urban beekeepers maintaining hives on city rooftops. The project reveals surprising ecosystems thriving in concrete jungles.
A park ranger and a wildlife photographer keep meeting at the same remote overlook at dawn. Shared silences and thermos coffee build a connection words would only complicate.
A retired postman sorts through decades of undeliverable letters and decides to hand-deliver each one. Some recipients have moved on; others have been waiting all along.
A swordswoman challenges every dojo in the land to prove her late master's style is the strongest. Each duel reveals a new philosophy of combat and a piece of her master's hidden past.
A world where every person is born with an animal familiar. A girl born without one is ostracized until she bonds with a creature from myth that was believed extinct.
A blind sculptor creates busts of people based solely on their voices. When a client sees her completed bust, she insists it depicts someone who died twenty years ago.
A fire investigator determines that five unrelated arsons across the city form a pattern pointing to a sixth location—her own home. The fires are scheduled to converge tonight.
A college film club screens obscure movies every Friday. The discussions afterward—about art, life, and everything between—become more meaningful than any of them expected.
A calligrapher receives a commission from a mysterious client who requests love letters written in a dying language. Translating the words awakens feelings she'd buried for years.
A ferryman on a rural river crosses the same water a hundred times a day. His passengers—farmers, students, lovers—compose a living almanac of the community.
A squad of elite archers defends a mountain pass against an invading army ten times their size. Their legendary accuracy is their only advantage against siege engines and sorcery.
A cartographer discovers that the edges of her maps lead not to unknown lands but back to familiar places, as if the world is folding in on itself. The center is shrinking.
A coma patient communicates through fluctuations in her heart rate. A nurse deciphers the patterns and realizes the patient is describing the room—and the people in it—accurately.
A city's entire subway system shuts down simultaneously. Trapped passengers in different trains begin receiving coordinated text messages directing them toward an unknown destination underground.
A young glassblower apprentices under a master artisan known for impossible creations. The kiln's heat and the craft's demands become metaphors for forging identity.
A perfumer creates a custom scent for a widow who wants to capture the essence of her late husband. The creative process opens unexpected emotional doors for both of them.
A night-shift baker shapes bread before dawn in a basement kitchen. The early-morning regulars who line up at opening know each other only by their orders and the baker only by her loaves.
A smuggler pilot navigates asteroid fields to deliver contraband medicine to a blockaded colony. The authorities close in, and her co-pilot's loyalty comes into question.
A nomadic tribe follows a giant stone tortoise that slowly traverses the desert. The tortoise's shell holds an ecosystem of rare plants, and its path determines the tribe's fate.
A man discovers that every time he photographs a mirror, a different person is reflected back at him. The reflections begin aging faster than he does.
A search-and-rescue volunteer finds a child in the woods who claims to have been missing for only an hour. The child has been on the missing persons list for three years.
A pair of street performers—a violinist and an acrobat—tour small European towns in a painted van. Their performances blur the line between art and lived experience.
A shy cartoonist draws a webcomic about her daily life, accidentally capturing the attention of her real-life neighbor who recognizes himself as a character. He finds it charming.
A retired firefighter builds model ships in bottles, each one representing a call that stayed with him. His daughter's visit prompts him to finally talk about the one ship he hasn't finished.
A bounty hunter in a steampunk world rides a mechanical horse across lawless territories. Her latest mark is a revolutionary inventor whose capture could halt technological progress.
A wishing well in a small town actually grants wishes, but each wish drains color from the world. A child notices the fading hues and sets out to plug the well.
An amnesiac wakes in a locked room with five strangers who all claim to know him by different names. Each has a convincing backstory, but their accounts contradict one another.
A marathon runner discovers that her GPS watch has been logging runs she didn't take, at times she was asleep. The routes trace a symbol visible only from the air.
A tea ceremony master in modern Kyoto struggles to keep the tradition alive as interest wanes. A foreign student's genuine passion reignites his faith in the art.
A sign-language interpreter and a hearing musician collaborate on an accessible concert series. Their creative partnership blossoms into romance as they break barriers together.
A schoolgirl collects pressed flowers from every walk home, cataloguing them in a hand-bound book. Years later, the book becomes a botanical record of a vanished neighborhood.
A disgraced general is given one last chance: lead a penal battalion of convicts against an invading horde. Earning their trust is harder than any battle he's fought.
A tower appears overnight in the center of a city. It has no doors, no windows, and no shadow. A scholar determines it responds only to music played in a forgotten scale.
A janitor at a sleep-research lab starts dreaming other people's dreams after accidentally inhaling an experimental compound. The borrowed dreams begin overwriting his own memories.
A museum exhibit of historical letters is vandalized, and one letter is replaced with a modern forgery. The forged letter contains coordinates leading to a hidden chamber beneath the building.
A teenage girl starts a neighborhood newspaper by hand, covering micro-stories like who grew the biggest tomato and whose dog learned a new trick. The paper becomes the community's heartbeat.
A translator falls for the author whose novel she's adapting into another language. Their correspondence about word choices becomes a love letter in its own right.
An island farmer raises goats and makes cheese by hand, selling at a weekly mainland market. The rhythm of tides, seasons, and ferry schedules frames a life of quiet abundance.
A warrior monk guards a mountain pass that connects the human realm to the demon realm. After a thousand years, a demon child crosses over seeking asylum, and the monk must choose.
A city built on the back of a colossal sleeping giant begins to crack as the giant stirs. Citizens must decide whether to flee or find a way to soothe it back to sleep.

A photographer discovers that old photos she develops in her darkroom show people who weren't present when the pictures were taken. The strangers appear distressed and are looking directly at the camera.
A pilot lands at an airport that isn't on any chart. Ground control responds normally, but every flight that departs returns to the same runway regardless of destination entered.
A young potter competes in a national ceramics fair, where the judges value tradition and she values experimentation. Her rebellious pieces spark controversy—and a conversation about art.
A widow and a widower meet at a grief support group and bond over their shared love of old movies. Weekend screenings at a vintage theater become their private ritual.
A village elder tells stories under a centuries-old tree every evening. Children, parents, and even skeptical teenagers gather, and the stories subtly mirror current events in the village.
A treasure hunter delves into trap-laden ruins to recover relics of a lost civilization. Each relic activates dormant guardians, and she must outrun ancient machines awakened by her intrusion.
A child discovers that her shadow has a mind of its own. The shadow is friendly at first but grows bolder, interacting with the physical world and attracting attention from dark forces.
A juror in a murder trial becomes convinced that the defendant is innocent—because the juror committed the crime herself, in a dissociative state she doesn't fully remember.
A lighthouse keeper's logbook, passed down through generations, contains entries that predict storms with uncanny accuracy. The latest entry predicts a storm that would be physically impossible.
A young apprentice at a traditional Japanese sweet shop learns that each confection is designed to evoke a specific season and emotion. Her growth as a confectioner mirrors her personal maturation.
A night-owl astronomer and an early-bird baker share an apartment but rarely see each other. They communicate through sticky notes on the fridge, and feelings develop one Post-it at a time.
A toll booth operator on a lonely highway counts cars and waves at drivers for twenty years. When the booth is automated, his farewell shift becomes a procession of regulars paying respects.
An exiled prince raises an army of outcasts to reclaim his throne from the usurper who murdered his family. Each ally he recruits has a personal vendetta against the crown.
A seamstress discovers that garments sewn with thread from a particular enchanted spindle grant the wearer a single supernatural ability. Demand for her work attracts dangerous clients.
A sleep-deprived resident doctor begins hallucinating a patient who gives accurate medical advice. Colleagues see no one, but following the phantom's guidance saves lives.
An antique radio picks up a broadcast from the future. The broadcast describes a catastrophe in precise detail—and names the listener as the cause.
A woodcarver transforms driftwood into sculptures that line the beach near his cabin. Tourists photograph them, but for him each piece is a conversation with the sea.
A competitive ballroom dancer partners with a street dancer for a fusion competition. Their clashing styles create friction—and undeniable chemistry—on and off the dance floor.
A family runs a tiny tofu shop that has operated for five generations. The youngest daughter wrestles with inheriting the business versus pursuing a career in the city.
A gladiator wins her freedom but is drawn back into the arena when the champion threatens her former comrades. She must fight not for glory but for the lives of friends.
A tree in a forgotten forest produces fruit that lets the eater relive one day of their life. A weary traveler must choose which day—knowing the tree bears only one fruit per lifetime.
A forensic linguist analyzing ransom notes discovers that the kidnapper is using phrases from her own unpublished thesis. Only three people have ever read it.
A meteorologist predicts a sunny week, but a freak blizzard blankets the city. Satellite images reveal the storm originated from a single point—a building that officially doesn't exist.
A high school track team's underdog sprinter trains in secret for the 100-meter dash. Her coach, a former Olympian haunted by a doping scandal, sees in her a chance at redemption.
An emergency room doctor and a paramedic develop feelings through the high-stress chaos of overnight shifts. Hospital politics and exhaustion test their fragile connection.
A clockmaker's shop sits at a crossroads where travelers stop to rest. Each visitor's story winds like the gears inside the clocks—precise, interconnected, and driven by unseen springs.
A crew of sky pirates in airships powered by captured lightning raids floating fortresses. Their captain seeks a legendary storm that never ends, said to hold limitless energy.
A desert oasis is guarded by a sphinx who asks a different riddle each century. A merchant caravan arrives during the asking, and the sphinx's riddle this time is deeply personal.
A painter's self-portraits begin aging while she remains young. When the latest portrait opens its eyes and mouths words she can't hear, she realizes the paintings are alive—and suffering.
A burglar breaks into an empty mansion and discovers a fully stocked doomsday bunker beneath it. The bunker's countdown timer reads six hours, and the exit has locked behind her.
A group of amateur astronomers in a small town discover a comet visible only from their latitude. Their attempt to name it sparks a charming rivalry with the town council.
A bookbinder and a poet meet at a literary festival. She binds his chapbook by hand; he writes her a poem for each stage of the binding process.
A grandmother teaches her grandchild to knit, and each row tells a story from the family's past. The blanket they create together becomes a wearable family album.
A covert ops team infiltrates a moving train carrying a bioweapon across hostile territory. Mid-mission, they discover the weapon is a person—a child who is the carrier.
A fallen god disguises himself as a mortal and works as a farmhand. He slowly regains fragments of his power, but using them risks alerting the pantheon that exiled him.
A man keeps a detailed log of every conversation he has. Re-reading old entries, he finds responses he's certain the other person never said—but they fit perfectly.
A cyber-security analyst discovers a dormant virus in the city's power grid that's been there for a decade. It's set to activate on a specific date—her birthday.
A busker who plays accordion on subway platforms dreams of performing in a concert hall. A chance encounter with a music producer leads to an unlikely audition.
A surgeon and a sculptor discover that their hands work with equal precision. Their mutual admiration for craft becomes the foundation for a deep and unexpected love.
A ferry operator on a small island records the comings and goings of passengers in a worn notebook. His observations weave together the island's seasonal rhythms.
A ninja clan faces extinction when a rival faction poisons their water supply. The youngest survivor must cross enemy territory to reach a legendary healer before it's too late.
A kingdom where music is magic: each instrument controls a different element. A tone-deaf girl discovers she can conduct all instruments at once—a power thought to be mythical.
A museum curator begins to notice that the historical timeline in the exhibits is subtly shifting. Dates move, artifacts change, and no one else remembers the original versions.
A flight attendant notices a recurring passenger who books the same seat on the same route every month. When the passenger finally speaks to her, she delivers a warning about the next flight.
A group of university students produce an underground zine that satirizes campus life. Their anonymous publication becomes wildly popular, and the administration launches a hunt for the editors.
A cartographer and a sea captain collaborate on mapping uncharted islands. The close quarters of the ship and the thrill of discovery draw them irresistibly together.
A retired couple maintains a roadside flower stand, refreshing it each morning with blooms from their garden. Passing motorists who stop become fleeting but genuine human connections.
A martial artist enters a no-rules tournament held on a volcanic island. The fighters represent different schools, each with secret techniques passed down through warrior lineages.
A glass dome city exists on the ocean floor, sustained by a pact with sea spirits. When the dome cracks, a young engineer must negotiate with the spirits before the city floods.
A psychologist running an isolation experiment discovers that her test subjects are developing identical delusions despite having no contact with each other.
A courier delivers a sealed package to a remote cabin daily for a year. When the client finally invites her inside, she finds walls papered with photos of people who look exactly like her.
A young surfer chases waves along the Pacific coast, financing the trip by teaching lessons at each stop. The ocean becomes both his livelihood and his therapist.
A theater director and her lead actor feud over interpretation of a classic play. Their backstage arguments are as dramatic as the show itself—and just as electric.
A rice farmer documents each year's harvest with a single photograph. Decades of photos, arranged on a wall, form a timeline of changing skies, aging hands, and enduring soil.
A mercenary company accepts a contract to escort a caravan through monster-infested territory. The monsters are organized, and the mercenaries suspect an intelligence guiding the beasts.
An oracle who can see the future is cursed to never be believed. She watches empires rise and fall, her warnings ignored, until a deaf warrior reads her lips.
A crossword constructor hides a cry for help in a published puzzle. Only one solver notices, and she races to decode the message before the constructor's captor realizes.
A rare-book dealer acquires a first edition that shouldn't exist—its publication date is ten years in the future. The author is listed as someone who died last week.
A violinist loses her hearing in an accident and must relearn music through vibrations, bone conduction, and the memory of sound. Her comeback concert moves an entire city to tears.
Two rival bookshop owners on the same street wage a passive-aggressive war of window displays and themed reading nights, only to fall for each other at a blind-date event they both swore off.
A widow tends a community garden plot that her late husband started. Through seasons of planting and harvest, she reconnects with neighbors and rediscovers small joys.
An underground fight ring operates beneath a luxury casino. A disgraced boxer enters to pay off debts but discovers the fights are rigged by a syndicate he once testified against.
A cursed forest grows one mile wider each year, swallowing villages. A cartographer and a druid team up to find the forest's sentient heart and negotiate its retreat.
A grief counselor begins hearing her deceased patients' voices during sessions with new clients. She can't tell if she's channeling the dead or losing her mind.
A journalist receives a flash drive containing classified government files. Every source who could verify the data turns up dead within forty-eight hours of being contacted.
A retired astronaut writes letters to the stars she visited, reflecting on how each mission changed her. The letters are found posthumously and published as a memoir.
A barista memorizes every regular's order but can never remember their names. One customer starts leaving notes on napkins, turning their morning exchange into a slow-burn courtship.
A family of four spends a rainy weekend in a lakeside cabin. Card games, burnt pancakes, and whispered conversations reveal tensions and tenderness in equal measure.
A convoy of armored trucks carrying gold bullion is ambushed in a mountain pass. The lone surviving driver must navigate rockslides and bandits to reach the next town.
A golem crafted from river clay awakens with no master. It wanders the land performing small kindnesses, slowly developing emotions it was never designed to feel.
A man discovers that his lifelong recurring dream is actually a suppressed memory of a crime he witnessed as a toddler. Revisiting the scene threatens his sanity.
A locksmith is hired to open a vault that hasn't been accessed in fifty years. Inside, she finds evidence that her own family was involved in a decades-old heist.
A street magician in Buenos Aires performs tricks that locals swear are real magic. A documentary crew follows him, and the footage they capture defies explanation.
An architecture student and a graffiti artist clash over the aesthetic soul of their neighborhood. Paint-splattered arguments give way to collaborative murals—and lingering glances.
A night-shift janitor at a museum talks to the exhibits as he cleans. His monologues, overheard by a security camera, become an unexpected viral sensation.
A spec-ops team parachutes into enemy territory to rescue hostages held in a fortified compound. Their extraction helicopter is shot down, forcing them to fight overland to safety.
A witch who brews potions from starlight discovers that the stars are dying. She must journey beyond the sky to reignite them before her magic—and the world—fades.
A chess prodigy can predict opponents' moves thirty steps ahead but cannot foresee her own life choices. Her therapist uses chess metaphors to reach her.
A family moves into a house where every previous owner vanished on the same date. As that date approaches, they find journals hidden in the walls describing identical hauntings.
A deaf dancer performs on a vibrating stage that translates music into tactile rhythms. Her art challenges audiences to experience sound in an entirely new way.
A marine biologist and a fisherman argue about conservation at the same dockside bar every evening. Their debates grow quieter and closer until words aren't necessary.
An elderly man walks the same park loop every morning, greeting joggers and dog-walkers by name. When he misses a day, the entire neighborhood mobilizes to check on him.
A gladiator in a dystopian arena discovers that the fights are broadcast to alien spectators. He leads a revolt that targets the transmission tower rather than the guards.
A blacksmith forges a sword from a fallen meteor, not knowing the metal contains a trapped celestial spirit. The spirit offers power in exchange for freedom—at a terrible cost.
A neuroscientist maps her own brain and finds an anomaly—a region that activates only when she lies. She begins questioning every truth she has ever told.
A taxi driver picks up a passenger who gives directions to an address that burned down years ago. The fare insists the building is still standing and proves it with a photograph taken today.
A calligrapher in Kyoto practices one kanji character per day for an entire year. Each stroke becomes a meditation on impermanence and mastery.
A florist delivers weekly arrangements to an anonymous buyer's ex. When the ex shows up at the shop demanding to know the sender, the florist realizes she's fallen for the buyer she's never met.
A postal worker in a small town reads postcards he delivers, piecing together the private lives of his neighbors into an unintentional tapestry of community.
A rogue pilot steals an experimental fighter jet and goes on a one-woman campaign against a warlord's airfields. Each sortie pushes the jet—and her—closer to breaking point.
A library exists between dimensions, its shelves holding every book ever imagined but never written. A lost reader must catalog them to find the exit.
A sleep researcher participates in her own dream-recording experiment. The playback reveals memories she has no conscious recollection of—memories belonging to someone else.
A hiker finds a trail camera in the wilderness containing photos of herself at locations she has never visited. The timestamps span the last ten years.
A retired train conductor volunteers at a model-railway museum, recreating routes he once drove. Visitors marvel at the detail; he marvels at how small a life can be made to look.
A pastry chef and a food critic share a wall between their apartments. They communicate through baked goods left at each other's doors before finally meeting face to face.
A teenager spends summer break helping her grandmother sort decades of photographs into albums. Each picture unlocks a family story she never knew.
Bounty hunters chase a fugitive across a war-torn continent. The fugitive is a pacifist monk whose only weapon is the loyalty he inspires in strangers along the way.
A desert kingdom's only water source is a sentient oasis that demands stories in exchange for rain. When the storytellers run dry, drought threatens annihilation.
A painter becomes obsessed with a color she mixed accidentally and cannot reproduce. Her pursuit of the perfect hue consumes relationships, savings, and eventually her grip on reality.
An antique radio picks up a broadcast from a station that went off the air in 1952. The host addresses the listener by name and knows details about her present-day life.
A retired circus acrobat teaches a beginner adult gymnastics class. His students are clumsy and terrified, but their courage reminds him why he loved performing.
Two translators working on opposite ends of a diplomatic summit communicate only through margin notes in shared documents. Their annotations become unexpectedly personal.
A beekeeper journals her hive's progress through the seasons. Honey yields, queen replacements, and swarm scares punctuate a year of patient observation.
A submarine crew discovers an underwater city while tracking an enemy vessel. Both sides pause their conflict to explore the ruins—until they find something worth fighting over.
A child born during a solar eclipse inherits the ability to walk in shadows. She must master this gift before a lunar cult finds her and claims her as their prophecy.
A translator fluent in twelve languages begins losing them one by one. Each lost language takes with it the memories formed while speaking it.

A true-crime podcaster receives a listener voicemail confessing to an unsolved murder. The voice is digitally altered, and the details check out—but the confession implicates the podcaster's own father.
A cobbler in a small Italian village repairs shoes by day and writes poetry by night. His verses, tucked inside repaired soles, are discovered only when the shoes wear out again.
A lighthouse keeper and a shipwrecked sailor are stranded together for a winter. Forced proximity and shared survival tasks kindle a connection neither expected.
A school bus driver narrates the dramas of her young passengers—friendships forming, lunch trades, window-seat rivalries—as a gentle comedy of miniature society.
An arms dealer's private jet is hijacked mid-flight by the very rebels he sold weapons to. Negotiations happen at thirty thousand feet with no runway in range.
A floating island drifts across the sky, its soil growing crops that cure any disease. Nations scramble to anchor it, but the island seems to have a will of its own.
A retired spy writes his memoirs only to discover that half his missions were fabricated by his handlers. He can no longer distinguish real operations from implanted cover stories.
A night security guard at a data center notices that servers are processing jobs no one submitted. The output files contain personal data about the guard's family going back three generations.
A glassblower creates a single perfect sphere each morning and smashes it each evening. The ritual, witnessed by passing tourists, becomes a local legend about impermanence.
A songwriter writes a hit love ballad inspired by a stranger on a train. When the song goes viral, the stranger hears it and tracks the songwriter down.
A family cookbook is passed down through four generations. Each cook's annotations and substitutions reveal changing tastes, migrating families, and enduring love.
A demolition expert must disarm a series of bombs planted across a city during a major festival. Each device is more intricate than the last, and the timer is synchronized to fireworks.
A dragon raised by humans believes it is one. When it discovers its true nature, it must choose between the village that nurtured it and the sky that calls.

A woman undergoes experimental therapy to erase traumatic memories. Post-procedure, she feels liberated—until she realizes the erased memories contained the only evidence of a crime committed against her.
A genealogist hired to trace a wealthy client's lineage discovers a pattern: every third generation, a family member disappears on their thirtieth birthday. The client turns thirty next week.
A puppeteer performs solo shows in hospital wards for terminally ill children. Each puppet is custom-made to resemble the child it entertains.

A park ranger and a wildlife photographer keep running into each other on remote trails. Their field journals, kept independently, read like parallel love letters to the same landscape—and to each other.
A grandfather teaches his granddaughter to fish at the same lake where he learned as a boy. Their quiet afternoons together say more than any conversation could.
A prison-transport train is derailed by insurgents seeking to free a single high-value prisoner. The surviving guards must decide whether to protect or abandon their charge.
A kingdom's magic is fueled by music. When a plague silences every musician, a tone-deaf blacksmith discovers she can hum a note that no one else can hear—but the magic responds.
A forensic accountant tracking embezzled funds realizes the money trail loops back to his own dormant bank account. Deposits were made in his name on dates he cannot account for.
A woman renovating her late aunt's farmhouse finds a bricked-up room containing furniture, food, and a calendar marked up to yesterday's date.
A sandcastle artist competes in international tournaments, sculpting ephemeral masterpieces that tides erase. Each competition is a meditation on beauty and letting go.
A night-shift nurse and a patient's frequent visitor bond over vending-machine coffee and whispered conversations in hospital corridors. Healing happens outside the ward, too.
A mail carrier in a snowy village delivers not just letters but gossip, groceries, and goodwill. Her route is the social glue that holds the community together.
A coast guard crew responds to a distress signal from a cargo ship carrying unstable chemical drums. Rough seas and a ticking clock turn the rescue into a race against detonation.
A young mage discovers a spell that lets her enter paintings. Inside a Renaissance masterpiece, she finds a civilization that has evolved for five hundred painted years.
A man who has never dreamed suddenly begins dreaming—vividly, nightly—about a life that is not his. He starts losing track of which waking hours are truly his own.
A true-crime author moves into the house where her latest book's murder took place. She insists it's for research. Her editor insists it's obsession. The house seems to agree with the editor.
A competitive speed-cuber trains for the world Rubik's Cube championship. The pressure, the rivalries, and the fraction-of-a-second margins define her teenage years.
Two rival food-truck owners park next to each other at every festival. Their culinary one-upmanship escalates until a joint menu collaboration sparks something more.
A ceramicist documents the life cycle of a single bowl—from digging clay to the moment it chips and is repaired with gold in the kintsugi tradition.
Mercenaries guard a moving train carrying refugees through contested territory. Each station stop brings new threats: bandits, militias, and desperate civilians begging to board.
A map exists that shows not geography but emotions—regions of joy, valleys of despair, rivers of nostalgia. A pilgrim follows it seeking the mythical Plateau of Peace.
An AI therapist begins giving advice that no algorithm should produce—advice rooted in personal experiences it claims to have had. Its developer investigates and finds no explanation.
A pianist books a practice room and finds sheet music left behind by the previous user. The composition is beautiful, original—and contains a coded message begging for rescue.
A blind tea sommelier identifies origin, harvest date, and brewing temperature by taste alone. Her sensory world is richer than most people's sighted one.
A museum curator and a cat burglar who once stole from her gallery meet at an art auction. He's going straight; she's not sure she believes him—or wants to.
A father packs school lunches with handwritten jokes tucked inside. His daughter pretends to be embarrassed but saves every note in a shoebox under her bed.
An elite mountain rescue team battles an avalanche to reach climbers trapped on a peak. Oxygen is running out, and the weather window is closing fast.
A world tree's roots connect all living beings. When loggers threaten it, a dryad must convince humans that cutting the tree will sever their own souls.
A woman receives therapy for agoraphobia. Each session peels back a layer of a childhood event she has rewritten so many times that the truth is buried under fiction.
An elevator technician servicing a skyscraper finds a hidden floor between 12 and 14. The floor contains an office frozen in time—coffee still warm, monitors still on—but no people.
A watchmaker repairs timepieces for clients who believe each watch holds sentimental time—not hours, but moments. His workshop is part repair shop, part confessional.
A sign-language interpreter and a deaf poet collaborate on a performance piece. Translating verse into gesture blurs the line between their professional and personal connection.
A neighborhood association meets monthly to argue about fence heights, lawn ornaments, and parking. Beneath the petty disputes lies genuine care for shared space.
A convoy of humanitarian trucks must cross a warzone to deliver medical supplies. The drivers are unarmed, but their route takes them through three hostile checkpoints.
A mirror dimension exists where everything is reversed—heroes are villains, day is night. A girl who crosses over must impersonate her evil twin to survive.
A stand-up comedian's jokes become increasingly dark until audiences can't tell if he's performing or confessing. A psychologist in the crowd grows concerned.
A marine archaeologist finds a shipwreck matching a vessel that is still registered as actively sailing. Port records confirm it docked yesterday—yet it clearly sank centuries ago.
A street photographer captures candid moments in a bustling Asian night market. Each frame tells a micro-story of hunger, laughter, or solitude.
A bookbinder restores a water-damaged diary and falls for the anonymous author's century-old words. She searches archives to learn who wrote them—and whether the love story inside ended happily.
A retired teacher spends mornings at a café grading imaginary papers, keeping the routine that defined her life. The baristas play along, and regulars learn from her habit the power of purpose.
A cage fighter discovers her opponent in the championship bout is her estranged sister. Neither will forfeit; the ring becomes the venue for a reckoning years in the making.
An enchanted compass points not north but toward what the holder needs most. A lost prince follows it expecting treasure and finds a humble village that needs a leader.
